title: Rule-Aware Cause-Specific Hazard Instead of One Binary Target
summary: Pit decisions are not one homogeneous process: dry-race mandatory compound usage, wet/intermediate crossover stops, and ordinary wear-driven stops follow different mechanisms.
rationale: Pit decisions are not one homogeneous process: dry-race mandatory compound usage, wet/intermediate crossover stops, and ordinary wear-driven stops follow different mechanisms. FIA rules remove the dry mandatory-compound logic once wets/inters are used, and Pirelli explicitly describes wide crossover windows around intermediates, so a single binary model may blur distinct hazard shapes.
implementation_hint: Infer coarse stop archetypes in train from current/next compound family and field-level wet/dry context, then train separate next-lap hazards for at least `dry-strategic`, `wet_to_dry`, and `dry_to_wet_or_reactive` regimes with a small gate. Recombine to final `P(PitNextLap=1)` and validate with grouped folds on `Year_Race` only.
expected_effect: Should reduce label noise on wet and transition laps, improving generalization more than another global tyre-age heuristic.
risk: Archetype inference can be noisy, especially without explicit weather; if the gate is weak this can over-segment sparse positive cases.
